persona.name: Skeptical Sarah
persona.description: Health-conscious but jaded by supplement marketing. Has tried AG1, random Amazon vitamins, maybe a greens powder. Nothing stuck. She reads labels, Googles ingredients, and doesn't trust influencer recommendations. She needs to see proof before she buys.

demographics.lifestyle: Busy professional, wants to be healthier but doesn't have time for complex routines

pain_points: Takes 4-5 separate supplements every morning (vitamins, probiotics, greens) and hates it
Spent money on AG1 and didn't notice a difference
Wants something that actually tastes good (not chalky/grassy)
what_converts_her: Real person showing real, subtle results (not dramatic transformation)
Hearing about experience after 2+ weeks (not instant miracle)
Price comparison showing value vs buying separately
Authentic UGC that doesn't feel like an ad
persona.content_consumption: Scrolls during morning coffee, lunch break, evening wind-down. Stops for relatable faces, not polished content

ad_character_match.should_look_like: Her or her friend. Late 20s to mid 30s. Not a fitness model. Real skin, real kitchen, real morning. Someone who has clearly tried other things and is genuinely surprised this works
ad_character_match.should_NOT_look_like: Influencer, model, fitness bro, anyone too put-together at 7am
